page: Microsoft Azure L48s_v4 is a storage and memory-optimized server instance belonging to the Lsv4 family. It is powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8573C processor with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The configuration features 48 virtual CPUs derived from 24 physical cores with 2 threads per core, managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. The CPU includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions for accelerated vector and matrix operations.

This instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a resource density of 8.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. It features a substantial local storage capacity of 11,519 GB and is Premium Storage capable, making it suitable for applications requiring large, high-speed local data volumes. No graphics processing units are included in this configuration.

Benchmark results indicate strong performance across multiple domains. In memory bandwidth testing, the instance achieved 3,700.79 GB/sec for cached small-block reads and 223.94 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads, placing it in the top tier of tested hardware. Compute performance is also robust, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 20,273.0 and a Passmark CPU score of 63,100.84. Database operations are highly efficient, demonstrated by a Passmark database score of 25,789.77 and a Redis SET throughput of 67.33 million operations per second. Additionally, the hardware supports rapid text and image processing, alongside strong performance in LLM inference for small and medium models.

Given its high local storage capacity and substantial memory-to-vCPU ratio, the L48s_v4 offers high resource density and qualitative cost efficiency for data-intensive workloads. It is well-suited for large-scale databases, high-throughput transactional systems, big data analytics, caching layers, and memory-intensive enterprise applications that benefit from localized high-capacity storage without the need for dedicated GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure L48s_v4 is a high-capacity storage and memory-optimized server instance within the Lsv4 family. Powered by a dedicated 4.2 GHz Intel Xeon 8573C processor, it delivers 48 vCPUs across 24 physical cores, supported by 384.0 GB of RAM and 11,519 GB of local storage. The architecture features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark data highlights strong performance, including a Passmark CPU score of 63,100.84, a Redis SET throughput of 67.33 million operations per second, and uncached memory read bandwidth of 223.94 GB/sec. Lacking a GPU, this instance is designed for high-throughput database operations, large-scale caching, and data-intensive enterprise workloads requiring massive local storage and high memory density.
og_description: Microsoft Azure L48s_v4 is an Lsv4-family server featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11,519 GB of local storage, optimized for high-throughput database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure L48s_v4 is a storage-optimized server with 48 Intel vCPUs, 384 GB RAM, and 11,519 GB of local storage for database workloads.
tagline: A high-density storage and memory-optimized platform featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processing and massive local storage for data-intensive enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of system memory providing 8.0 GB of RAM per core
11,519 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 support for accelerated vector and matrix operations
Strong database performance with 67.33 million Redis SET operations per second
Optimized for large-scale databases, caching layers, and big data analytics
